title: Feature-Based Clean Architecture
summary: Structure the frontend around product capabilities, then keep domain and infrastructure boundaries explicit.
purpose: This page turns the architecture principles into a concrete folder model that works for React and React Native features.
rule: Use modules for feature delivery, domains for business concepts, infrastructure for technical adapters, presentation for shared UI, app for composition, and shared for stable cross-cutting utilities.
whenToUse: Adding a new screen, flow, or reusable feature capability.
Moving duplicated logic out of a component.
Creating code that will be reused by multiple product areas.
Explaining where a new file belongs during review.
whenNotToUse: Do not promote code to shared just because a second file imports it once.
Do not create domain folders for purely visual concepts.
Do not create infrastructure folders inside features when the concern is shared by the app.

recommended.title: Feature-based structure
recommended.code:
src/
  app/
    navigation/
    providers/
    routes/
  modules/
    invoices/
      screens/
      ui/
      hooks/
      mappers/
      tests/
  domains/
    billing/
      core/
      use-cases/
      ports/
  infrastructure/
    http/
    storage/
    analytics/
    billing/
  presentation/
    components/
    tokens/
  shared/
    result.ts
    date-format.ts

avoid.title: File-type buckets
avoid.code:
src/
  components/
  hooks/
  services/
  types/
  utils/
  screens/

why: File-type buckets look simple at first but hide ownership. Feature-based clean architecture keeps daily product work close to the feature while protecting business rules from UI, backend, storage, and platform changes. The folder is not the goal; the dependency direction is. Features orchestrate use cases, domains express decisions, and infrastructure handles technical details.
checklist: The folder name communicates product ownership.
Reusable UI is in presentation, not inside a random feature.
Shared utilities are stable, tested, and domain-neutral.
Domain code does not import screens or components.
Features call domain use cases instead of importing infrastructure directly.
